focus: JSONL append-only safety under concurrency (collab ledgers + child-agents.jsonl)

findings.summary: Collab ledgers are currently parse-clean under multi-commander writes, but safety is policy-based (not enforcement-based).
evidence: all ledgers end with newline; max line sizes <=1367B (well below 4KB)
writers observed from 5 commanders across the same ledgers
findings.risk: No writer lock, no append helper, and no schema gate means correctness depends on every writer behaving perfectly.

findings.summary: child-agents.jsonl experienced a real integrity incident consistent with concurrent writer clobber/truncation.
evidence: child-agents.jsonl contains telemetry_integrity_warning event stating file was truncated/restarted during concurrent child writes (line 99)
re-appended recovery records are present with note "re-appended after concurrent telemetry truncation/overwrite" (lines 213-222)
additional reconciled/reconstructed backfill events exist (e.g., lines 324-330, 351)
findings.risk: Causal ordering and launch-proof confidence are degraded after reconstruction; append-only invariant was violated in practice.

findings.summary: Code/docs advertise O_APPEND semantics, but there is no concrete cross-process append primitive or lock discipline.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh:797 only documents O_APPEND guidance in protocol text
bin/stampede.sh:956 initializes child-agents.jsonl with mode "w" (truncate-on-open hazard on re-init/restart)
search across bin/agents/skills/run scripts found no flock/lockf/fcntl-based serialization helper
findings.risk: Any process opening ledger in write mode or performing buffered multi-write appends can break invariants under contention.

proposed_fixes.change: Introduce one shared append_jsonl() helper used by commander/squad/worker paths for ALL ledgers.
details: Optionally lock a sidecar .lock with fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX) for process serialization
Emit exactly one JSON-serialized line via os.write(... + b"\n")

proposed_fixes.change: Make child-agents.jsonl initialization idempotent and non-destructive.
details: Replace open(...,"w") seeding (bin/stampede.sh:956) with create-if-absent semantics
Never truncate existing ledgers during run or restart paths
If file exists, append commander_registered only when not already present for this run/commander
Track and alert on ledger size regression (shrink => truncation)
Track per-ledger monotonic append count and last byte offset
Fail launch-proof gate to partial if integrity canary trips

proposed_fixes.change: Enforce minimal schema envelope on append.
details: Require event_id, writer_id, ts, run_id, commander_id, event for every write
Reject/redirect malformed records to quarantine ledger instead of polluting primary ledger
Keep reader tolerant, but make writer strict

ship_gate_recommendation: HOLD for launch-proof claims until P0 fixes land; collab bus can remain usable but not trust-grade under heavy concurrency.
